rulings.Case Title: ANATOLE GEICHE VS. JOHN G HANLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Set Aside Default; Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT JOHN HANLIN Motion To Set Aside Default. Motion to set aside default is granted. Although plaintiff properly effectuated substitute service, defendant did not have actual notice in time to defend the action. Relief is granted under CCP section 473.5. Defendant must file his answer no later than July 27, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HINES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT NOP 560 MISSION LLC, and, HINES INTERESTS L. P. DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the Third Cause of Action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing, plaintiff must plead the written contract word for word, or generally according to its legal intentment and effect, or attach a copy and incorporate it by reference. Insufficient facts alleged regarding the express agreements to determine the implied covenants. The Eighth Cause of Action for recission must specify the relationships of the parties, the existence of a contract, and the contractual obligation that would permit a recission claim. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MYDRA MCGARR VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant ratified the tortious conduct of Samuel. Plaintiff reported Samuel's threats of violence to her employer (Plaintiff's deposition 89:13-90:9; 70:5-71:13), Samuel was known to be taking drugs that affected her behavior (Lopez deposition 26:3-11 and McElroy deposition 43:9-16), and the employer failed to intervene with the exception of one talk by Carrigan. (Plaintiff's depositoin 89:13-90:9 and Carrigan depostion 107:1-110:9). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA RABINOVICH VS. COUNTRIWIDE HOME LOANS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2009, line 12, Order to Show Cause re: Preliminary Injunction. Preliminary Injunction is denied. Plaintiff fails to show irreparable harm. Plaintiff does not allege that the property is her residence and no allegation in the moving papers that money damages are inadequate. =(302/CWW)
